title: Data Science Document
text:
Answers to questions

I think being a data scientist is about communicating analyses of data to a variety of disciplines with no assumptions regarding statistical background.
My initial understanding of a statistician is more in line with what I think of as a data scientist, so I think more generally a statistician develops algorithms and methods that are then used by data scientists, eg. LASSO, Bayesian inference, etc. In this respect, I think every data scientist is a(n applied) statistician, but not vice-versa.
Given what I’ve read in this week’s materials, I think I am more akin to a data scientist than a statistician, despite my strong mathematics background. I am more interested in the interpretation of results than finding or strengthening new or existing methods.



Code Sample
First we run the instructed code:

y <- density(iris$Petal.Width)

Then we run code to examine the format of y

class(y)
